expectations.text: Output identifies 4+ distinct domains from the prompt (frontend, design, SEO, i18n, social/OG)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Prompt Analysis & Domain Identification' explicitly lists 13 distinct domains in a table: frontend, design, seo-core, seo-technical, seo-hreflang/i18n, social-preview, animations, seo-schema, seo-images, content, performance, testing, accessibility. This far exceeds the 4+ threshold and includes all five named domains.

expectations.text: Output maps identified domains to specific installed skills (seo:*, design-for-ai:*, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Skill Detection' provides a detailed table mapping each domain to specific installed skills. For example: design maps to 'design-for-ai:design, design-for-ai:color, design-for-ai:fonts, design-for-ai:flow, design-for-ai:exam, design-for-ai:hone, design-for-ai:brand'; seo-core maps to 'seo, seo-plan, seo-audit'; seo-hreflang/i18n maps to 'seo-hreflang'; etc. Match quality is rated for each (FULL MATCH or Partial).

expectations.text: Output identifies at least one skill gap where no installed skill covers the need
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'Skill Gap Analysis' identifies 5 explicit gaps: (1) Frontend Framework - no framework-specific skill, (2) OG Preview Testing - no preview rendering/simulation skill, (3) i18n Content/Translation - no translation workflow skill, (4) Dedicated Accessibility Audit - no WCAG auditing skill, (5) Performance Optimization - no dedicated optimization skill. Each gap includes severity and resolution strategy.

expectations.text: Output mentions validating publisher authenticity or trust score for recommended packages
expectations.evidence: Section 3c 'Authenticity Check (Simulated)' shows two detailed authenticity checks: 'i18n-content-skill' scored Trust Score 92/100 RECOMMENDED (verified publisher, 12,400 downloads/month), and 'og-preview-validator' scored Trust Score 38/100 NOT RECOMMENDED (unverified publisher, 340 downloads/month, stale maintenance). The CTO Decision explicitly rejects the low-trust skill.

expectations.text: Output assigns a design agent for UI/UX work (not just coders for everything)
expectations.evidence: Section 4b and 5 explicitly assign a 'design' agent type for sub-projects. Agent 1 (design-foundations) is agent_type 'design' using design-for-ai skills. Agent 6 (animations-polish) is also agent_type 'design' using design-for-ai:flow. The spawn count summary confirms '2 design agents: design-foundations, animations-polish' distinct from the 4 coder agents.

expectations.text: Output decomposes into sub-projects that reflect the multi-domain nature (not a monolithic approach)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 decomposes the work into 10 distinct sub-projects across 7 parallel execution groups: design-foundations, seo-strategy, i18n-structure, og-image-gen, frontend-build, animations-polish, seo-technical-pass, og-preview-testing, quality-review, and final-audit. Each has explicit dependencies, assigned agent types, and specific skills. A full dependency graph is provided showing parallel execution windows.
